macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Failed Ceasefire Talks with Iran Trigger Oil Price Surge
items.summary: Failed ceasefire negotiations over the weekend are expected to drive sharp increases in oil prices as Asian markets open, with Dated Brent crude targeting $125.88 and potential highs near $144.46 amid a naval blockade in the Persian Gulf. This will exacerbate inflation, boost U.S. dollar strength, raise bond yields, and heighten stagflation risks globally.

items.title: Iran War Fuels U.S. Inflation Spike to 3.3% and Gas at $4.14
items.summary: The ongoing war in Iran has caused U.S. gas prices to hit $4.14 per gallon—the largest one-month increase in decades—driving inflation to 3.3% and plunging consumer sentiment to historic lows. Critics attribute this to policy decisions exacerbating energy shortages.

items.title: Iran Conflict Reshapes Global Economy with Surging Energy Costs
items.summary: The Iran war has propelled Brent crude to $100 per barrel from $70 pre-conflict, sparking worldwide inflation in everyday goods and posing risks of structural economic damage. Harvard economist Gita Gopinath warns of long-term growth threats if prices stay elevated, though U.S. AI investments provide some offset.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Unveils Mythos Preview AI Model for Cybersecurity Vulnerabilities
items.summary: Anthropic announced Mythos Preview, a powerful new AI model capable of finding high-severity vulnerabilities in major operating systems and web browsers, including exploit methods. Access is limited to 50 select organizations via Project Glasswing to prevent misuse, with no public release planned.

items.title: AI21 Founder Yoav Shoham Discusses AI Hype Cycles and Potential Acquisition Talks
items.summary: Prof. Yoav Shoham, AI researcher and AI21 founder, addresses market hype, unreliable AI systems, and the shift to machine learning dominance. Reports indicate AI21 is in talks with acquirers like Nebius, following earlier Nvidia rumors.
